cards.title: You just ate — digestion mode
cards.body: Insulin is elevated and your body is absorbing nutrients. Keep sipping water. Avoid snacking to let insulin settle.

cards.title: Digestion winding down
cards.body: Stick to water or unsweetened tea. A short 10–15 minute walk can help glucose levels settle.

cards.title: Early fast — keep sipping
cards.body: Insulin is falling. Plain water, sparkling water, black coffee, or plain tea are all fine and won't break your fast.

cards.title: Coffee? Keep it black
cards.body: Caffeine is okay. Cream, milk, sugar, and sweeteners with calories will break your fast. A pinch of cinnamon is fine.

cards.title: Consider electrolytes
cards.body: You're dipping into glycogen stores and flushing water. A pinch of salt in water, or an electrolyte drink without sugar, can prevent headaches and fatigue.

cards.title: Hunger comes in waves
cards.body: Hunger typically peaks and passes. Drink water, take a short walk, or change activity. If you feel dizzy or unwell, end your fast gently.

cards.title: Entering fat-burning
cards.body: Your body is now primarily burning fat. Keep hydrating. Light activity is great; avoid intense workouts unless you're adapted.

cards.title: Plan a gentle break-fast
cards.body: Start small: bone broth, a handful of nuts, plain yogurt, or a piece of fruit. Avoid refined carbs, big portions, and sugary drinks on an empty stomach.

cards.title: Deep ketosis and autophagy
cards.body: Extended fasts carry more risk. Listen to your body — dizziness, heart palpitations, or extreme fatigue mean end the fast now.

allowed.name: Water
allowed.notes: Plain, sparkling, or mineral water. The ideal fasting beverage.

allowed.name: Black coffee
allowed.notes: No sugar, no milk, no cream. A small amount of cinnamon is fine.

allowed.name: Plain tea (black/green/herbal)
allowed.notes: Unsweetened. Herbal teas without added calories are great.

allowed.name: Zero-calorie electrolytes
allowed.notes: Pinch of sea salt in water, or sugar-free electrolyte mixes. Especially helpful after 12h.

allowed.name: Apple cider vinegar (splash)
allowed.notes: A small splash in water. Can blunt hunger. Rinse your mouth afterward to protect enamel.

allowed.name: A slice of lemon or lime
allowed.notes: The few calories from a slice are negligible. Whole juice is not allowed.

allowed.name: Diet soda / artificial sweeteners
allowed.notes: Unlikely to break a fat-loss fast, but can trigger cravings and an insulin response in some people.

allowed.name: Sugar-free gum
allowed.notes: Small sugar alcohol load. Probably fine, but skip it if your goal is strict autophagy.

allowed.name: Bone broth
allowed.notes: Has ~30–50 calories and protein. Technically breaks a strict fast but can be useful on longer fasts.

allowed.name: BCAAs / amino acid drinks
allowed.notes: Small protein load that spikes insulin. Avoid if you want autophagy; okay for gym fasts.

allowed.name: Milk or cream in coffee
allowed.notes: Even a splash contains lactose and fat — breaks the fast.

allowed.name: Fruit juice
allowed.notes: High in sugar; spikes insulin quickly.

allowed.name: Protein shake
allowed.notes: Protein raises insulin and stops the fasted state.

allowed.name: Any solid food
allowed.notes: If in doubt, solid food ends the fast.

allowed.name: Alcohol
allowed.notes: Adds calories and stresses the liver during a fast.

allowed.name: Gummy or sweetened vitamins
allowed.notes: Added sugars break the fast. Plain capsule vitamins with water are fine.
